patches.find: Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (JGR 104:10111–10118) measured damping decrease (Q increase) and frequency increase during solar proton events at Arrival Heights — i.e., cavity-Q enhancement, not suppression. Pazos et al. 2019 (JASTP 193:105091) reported statistically significant amplitude increases at Coeneo during moderate-to-severe storms.
patches.replace: The most direct storm-time evidence is Pazos et al. 2019 (JASTP 193:105091), which reports statistically significant first-mode amplitude increases at Coeneo during moderate-to-severe geomagnetic storms (Dst ≲ −70 nT) — enhancement, not suppression. A distinct but related disturbance class, solar proton events (SPEs), shows the same direction: Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (JGR 104:10111–10118) measured damping decrease (Q increase) and frequency increase during SPEs at Arrival Heights — cavity-Q enhancement. (SPE and CME-driven geomagnetic storms differ mechanistically — proton precipitation deepens to mesospheric altitudes while CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region — so SPE direction is supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour.)
patches.rationale: EXP-232 (ISS-1451 PATCH A): Promotes Pazos et al. 2019 (genuine CME-driven G-class storms at Coeneo) to primary storm-time enhancement citation. Demotes Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (solar proton events at Arrival Heights) to 'distinct but related disturbance class', explicitly qualifying it as SPE evidence. Adds mechanistic parenthetical (proton precipitation mesospheric coupling vs CME-storm D-region coupling) to pre-empt dome defender category-error attack. Verified unique before-string.

patches.find: the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 paper often cited in the storm-time SR literature actually reports cavity-Q enhancement (damping decrease, frequency increase) during SPEs, not suppression.
patches.replace: the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 paper often cited in storm-time SR discussions for its cavity-Q observations during high-energy particle precipitation actually reports enhancement (damping decrease, frequency increase) during SPEs — and the genuine-CME-storm evidence (Pazos et al. 2019, paragraph 1) likewise reports enhancement, not suppression.
patches.rationale: EXP-232 (ISS-1451 PATCH B): Clarifies that S&F 1999 is cited for its specific cavity-Q observational finding (during high-energy particle precipitation) rather than framing it as a storm-time SR study. Adds cross-reference to Pazos et al. 2019 as the genuine-CME-storm evidence. Prevents dome defender from arguing S&F is being miscategorized as storm-time evidence. Verified unique before-string.
